skill.name: cto-advisor
skill.description: Technical leadership guidance for engineering teams, architecture decisions, technology strategy, technical debt, team scaling, and engineering metrics.
skill.summary: Plan engineering strategy, team growth, architecture decisions, and technical debt work.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 64 potential issues, but the high weak-crypto and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from business terminology, tables, and templates. The only confirmed risk is documented execution of bundled local Python scripts; reviewed scripts show no subprocess, network, environment, or file write behavior. No evidence found for prompt injection or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Documented Local Script Execution
low_findings.description: The skill tells users to run bundled Python calculators from SKILL.md. The commands are fixed local script invocations, and reviewed scripts show no subprocess, network, environment, or file write behavior. This is a low operational risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct command examples exist in SKILL.md, and the target scripts are local Python calculators. Review found no command composition, subprocess calls, or network exfiltration paths.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and Ruby backtick alerts map to markdown templates, business terminology, metrics tables, or fenced examples. No evidence found that these locations perform cryptography, system discovery, or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain non-executable documentation, scoring matrices, role labels, or ordinary calculations. No dangerous API or malicious data flow was found in the reviewed context.

content.user_title: Guide CTO Strategy and Engineering Decisions
content.value_statement: Engineering leaders need consistent methods for debt, architecture, metrics, and team growth decisions. This skill provides CTO frameworks, templates, and local calculators for practical planning.
actual_capabilities: Guides technical debt assessment with a bundled analyzer script.
Builds hiring and team structure plans with a scaling calculator.
Provides ADR templates and architecture governance examples.
Defines engineering metrics, DORA targets, dashboards, and OKR examples.
Supports technology and vendor evaluation with scoring and PoC templates.
limitations: Does not connect to live repositories, ticket systems, or metrics tools.
Python calculators use built-in assumptions unless users adapt the inputs.
Provides strategic guidance, not legal, financial, or compliance approval.
Does not replace leadership judgment or stakeholder validation.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Technical Debt
use_cases.description: Assess debt categories, estimate risk, and turn findings into a practical reduction roadmap.

use_cases.title: Plan Engineering Growth
use_cases.description: Estimate hiring needs, role mix, team structure, budget impact, and scaling risks.
use_cases.target_user: VPs of engineering and startup founders

use_cases.title: Standardize Architecture Decisions
use_cases.description: Use ADR templates and evaluation matrices to compare options and document tradeoffs.
use_cases.target_user: Staff engineers and architecture review boards

prompt_templates.title: Assess a Team
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of current engineering health.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the CTO advisor skill to review my engineering team. Focus on technical debt, metrics, team structure, and the next three actions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Debt Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning for a legacy system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess technical debt for this system profile and create a 90-day plan with priorities, risks, owners, and success metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate a Technology
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these technology options using the evaluation framework. Include criteria weights, tradeoffs, PoC plan, decision risks, and an ADR summary.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Scaling Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a four-quarter engineering scaling plan from current headcount to target headcount. Include roles, budget, team topology, metrics, and risks.

output_examples.input: Assess technical debt for a legacy commerce platform with low coverage, high coupling, and outdated deployment practices.
output_examples.output: A prioritized debt report with category scores, critical risks, a phased remediation roadmap, and executive recommendations.

output_examples.input: Plan growth from 12 to 40 engineers over four quarters.
output_examples.output: A hiring plan with quarterly roles, management ratios, team structure, budget range, onboarding risks, and mitigation steps.

output_examples.input: Choose between Kubernetes, ECS, and Nomad for a growing microservices platform.
output_examples.output: A weighted comparison, PoC checklist, decision rationale, operational risks, and an ADR-ready recommendation.

best_practices: Provide real team size, delivery goals, constraints, and risk tolerance.
Validate calculator assumptions before using recommendations for budgets or hiring.
Convert outputs into owned actions with dates, metrics, and review cadence.
anti_patterns: Do not treat framework scores as final decisions without stakeholder review.
Do not use generic benchmarks without adjusting for company stage and domain.
Do not run scripts from an untrusted copy of the skill without review.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan technical debt work, team scaling, architecture decisions, engineering metrics, and technology evaluation.

faq.question: Does it run external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found of external service calls. The bundled scripts perform local calculations from provided or example data.

faq.question: Can it replace a CTO?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured guidance and templates for leaders who still own the final decisions.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it useful for startups?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes team scaling, hiring ratios, debt prioritization, and technology selection guidance for growing teams.

faq.question: What should users review before running scripts?
faq.answer: Users should review the local Python files, confirm inputs, and check assumptions against their organization.
